text.title: Subagent prompt file structure
text.summary: Every prompt file under `prompts/` follows the same shape so the orchestrator can dispatch subagents uniformly and the user can audit prompts in version control.

section.title: Required sections

list.item: `Inputs` — the parameter block the orchestrator must pass.
`Workflow` — numbered steps, concrete enough to execute without further clarification.
`Constraints` — explicit do-not list (no push, no destructive writes, no out-of-scope edits).
`Output` — what the subagent returns to the orchestrator (report shape, files written, exit codes).
`Done condition` — observable check that lets the orchestrator decide success vs failure.

section.reference: Skeleton in the exact required shape.

section.title: Spawn message format
section.p: The orchestrator's spawn message is small: it tells the subagent which file to read and supplies the per-run parameters. The subagent reads the file with its own Read tool — no inline instruction text.

example.code:
Read <abs path>/prompts/05-task-executor.md and execute it with these inputs:
feature_folder: <abs path>/<sdd>/features/todo/<slug>/ repo_path: <abs path>/<repo> default_branch: <branch> worktree_root: /tmp/wt-<ticket-id>

rule.statement: A prompt file MUST NOT depend on conversation context outside its `Inputs` block. Every fact the subagent needs is either inline, in an explicitly referenced file, or computable from the inputs.
rule.rationale: The subagent has no access to the parent transcript. Any implicit dependency is silent corruption waiting to happen.

section.title: Idempotency where reasonable
section.p: Read-only subagents (analyzer, reviewer, verifier) MUST be safely re-runnable. Write-side subagents (executor, fix-applier, deployer) are not idempotent and MUST detect and refuse a partial-state re-run rather than silently double-applying.
